title: Mustardy Canadian Ice Cream Poutine
description: A rich and tangy ice cream poutine dish with a Canadian twist, featuring a mustardy flavor and a show-stopping tempering-chocolate topping. This plant-based-friendly dessert is sure to impress.

ingredients.name: Mustard glaze
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, using whole-grain mustard

ingredients.name: Dark chocolate wafers
ingredients.notes: at least 70% cocoa solids, dairy-free option available

ingredients.name: Fried pastry sticks
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, plant-based option recommended

ingredients.name: Fried Shallots
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, plant-based option recommended

ingredients.name: Fried eggplant
ingredients.notes: sliced and breaded, dairy-free option available

ingredients.name: Fried cauliflower
ingredients.notes: broken into florets and breaded, dairy-free option available

ingredients.name: Soy milk or other non-dairy milk

instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Cut the fried pastry sticks into 1-inch pieces and sprinkle them on the baking sheet.
Bake the pastry pieces in the oven for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown.
Meanwhile, prepare the mustard glaze by whisking together the mustard, soy milk, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
Dip the fried pastry sticks in the mustard glaze and set them aside to cool.
In a separate bowl, melt the chocolate wafers in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Stir in the granulated sugar and water to temper the chocolate.
Dip the cooled pastry sticks in the chocolate mixture and coat evenly.
Place the chocolate-coated pastry sticks on a wire rack to set.
In a separate pan, heat the caramel sauce over low heat.
Add the fried shallots and eggplant to the caramel sauce and stir to coat.
Add the fried cauliflower to the caramel sauce and stir to coat.
To assemble the poutine, place a scoop of the plant-based vanilla ice cream on a plate or bowl.
Top the ice cream with the chocolate-coated pastry sticks, followed by the fried vegetables and a drizzle of caramel sauce.
